name: Australia
hoverText: Mandatory climate-related disclosure under the Corporations Act and AASB S2 started on 1 Jan 2025 and is being phased in across three reporting groups; ASIC RG 280 explains scope and administration.

name: Austria
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Bangladesh
hoverText: A Bangladesh Bank sustainability/climate disclosure guideline page was identified, but detailed scope, ISSB alignment, and effective dates were not accessible in the reviewed sources; jurisdiction-wide status remains unspecified.

name: Belgium
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Bolivia
hoverText: IFRS sources show adoption in progress: Bolivia's national standard setter approved ISSB adoption, but the regulatory pronouncement making sustainability-related reporting mandatory was still pending; mandatory date remains unspecified.

name: Botswana
hoverText: No national mandatory ISSB-style regime was identified. Current use appears guidance-based, with ESG reporting guidance and government reference to 2024 Sustainability Disclosure Guidance and an upcoming national ESG strategy.

name: Brazil
hoverText: Brazil has a mandatory ISSB-based regime. Voluntary use began from FY2024 and local CBPS standards become mandatory for listed entities from FY2026; major financial institutions also face phased CBPS requirements.

name: Bulgaria
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: California
hoverText: California has the broadest current state-level corporate climate disclosure regime in this set. SB 253 requires annual Scope 1 and 2 reporting from 2026 and Scope 3 from 2027 for qualifying entities doing business in California; SB 261 separately requires climate-related financial-risk reporting.

name: Canada
hoverText: Canada's CSSB finalized CSDS 1 and CSDS 2 in Dec 2024, but they are voluntary unless mandated by legislation or a regulator. The CSA said in Apr 2025 it was pausing work on a new mandatory climate rule, while material climate-risk disclosure still applies under securities law.

name: Chile
hoverText: Chile already had phased TCFD/SASB-based reporting under CMF Rule 461. CMF Rule 519 shifts the regime toward IFRS S1/S2 for annual periods starting in 2026, to be reported in 2027.

name: China
hoverText: China's regime remains in progress. The Ministry of Finance system is based on IFRS S1/S2 but localized: the Basic Standard (Trial) was issued in Nov 2024 and a climate standard exposure draft followed in Apr 2025, with broader architecture targeted by 2030.

name: Czech Republic
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Denmark
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Estonia
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Finland
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: France
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Germany
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Greece
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Hungary
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: India
hoverText: India uses SEBI's BRSR framework for listed companies, with mandatory BRSR for the top 1,000 listed entities from FY2022-23 after a voluntary FY2021-22 period; SEBI added the BRSR Core assurance/value-chain framework in 2023.

name: Indonesia
hoverText: Indonesia currently combines OJK sustainable-finance reporting rules with an ISSB-based standard-setting roadmap. OJK Regulation 51/2017 already requires sustainability reporting for financial institutions, issuers, and public companies, while IAI's roadmap moves the jurisdiction toward IFRS S1/S2-style standards.

name: Ireland
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Israel
hoverText: No fully mandated ISSB-style economy-wide regime was identified in the reviewed official sources. Current use appears to rely on voluntary ESG disclosure recommendations and policy positions; climate-specific mandatory details remain unspecified.

name: Italy
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Japan
hoverText: Japan already requires sustainability information in annual securities reports under a 2023 FSA rule aligned to the TCFD pillars. SSBJ issued Japanese sustainability disclosure standards in Mar 2025, but economy-wide mandatory application remains under consideration.

name: Kenya
hoverText: Kenya's ICPAK roadmap adopts IFRS S1/S2. Reporting is permitted from 1 Jan 2024, then becomes mandatory in phases from 2027 for public interest entities, 2028 for large non-PIEs, and 2029 for SMEs.

name: Latvia
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Lithuania
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Malaysia
hoverText: Malaysia has a phased mandatory IFRS S1/S2 regime under the NSRF and Bursa Malaysia listing-rule amendments. Climate-first reporting begins for annual periods starting 1 Jan 2025, 2026, and 2027 by group, while full IFRS S1/S2 reporting starts in 2027, 2028, and 2030 respectively.

name: Mexico
hoverText: Mexico has mandated ISSB standards for most supervised issuers through CNBV changes to the CUE. The rules took effect on 29 Jan 2025, and first sustainability-related financial information is due in 2026 for FY2025.

name: Netherlands
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: New Jersey
hoverText: No broad economy-wide corporate climate disclosure statute was identified. New Jersey currently has a sectoral insurance disclosure regime: NJDOBI requires certain licensed insurers to respond to the TCFD-aligned Climate Risk Disclosure Survey.

name: New York
hoverText: No broad New York corporate climate disclosure law of general application was identified. Current requirements are sectoral and supervisory: DFS final guidance for insurers and separate banking/mortgage guidance expects climate-risk governance, risk management, and disclosure development with reference to TCFD-style approaches.

name: New Zealand
hoverText: New Zealand's climate-related disclosure regime is mandatory for climate reporting entities under the FMC Act for financial years beginning on or after 1 Jan 2023, using NZ CS 1-3; GHG assurance is required for periods ending on or after 27 Oct 2024.

name: Nigeria
hoverText: Nigeria's FRC roadmap adopts IFRS S1/S2 and future ISSB standards. Application is permitted from 1 Jan 2024, then becomes mandatory from 1 Jan 2028 for public interest entities and from 1 Jan 2030 for SMEs; updated guidance was issued in 2026.

name: Pakistan
hoverText: Pakistan has mandated IFRS S1 and IFRS S2 through an SECP order under a three-phase rollout. Phase I starts for annual periods beginning on or after 1 Jul 2025, followed by 2026 and 2027 phases.

name: Philippines
hoverText: The Philippines still uses SEC sustainability reporting guidelines for publicly listed companies, while IFRS-based PFRS S1/PFRS S2 adoption is progressing through domestic standard-setting; mandatory ISSB-style implementation remains in progress.

name: Poland
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Portugal
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Romania
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Slovakia
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Slovenia
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: South Africa
hoverText: South Africa currently relies mainly on guidance rather than a hard mandatory ISSB filing rule. JSE sustainability/climate disclosure guidance is in use, and the FSCA expects future alignment with IFRS S1/S2, but final mandatory timing remains unspecified.

name: South Korea
hoverText: South Korea remains in progress. The KSSB exposure draft is based on IFRS S1/S2 with an additional local policy-disclosure layer; staged adoption after 2026 is under consideration and final mandatory timing remains to be determined.

name: Spain
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Sri Lanka
hoverText: Sri Lanka issued SLFRS S1 and SLFRS S2, which incorporate IFRS S1/S2. Voluntary use was allowed from 1 Jan 2024, and mandatory application began from 1 Jan 2025 for the top 100 CSE listed companies, broadening in 2026 and 2027.

name: Sweden
hoverText: EU CSRD/ESRS is the operative baseline. First-wave entities reported for FY2024; later waves are delayed under the EU's 2025 'stop-the-clock' measure, moving most remaining large-company reporting to FY2027 and listed SME reporting to FY2028.

name: Taiwan
hoverText: Taiwan's FSC released a roadmap for listed companies to align with IFRS Sustainability Disclosure Standards. Mandatory application begins with FY2026 and is phased by company size, with reporting in 2027, 2028, and 2029.

name: Tanzania
hoverText: Tanzania has adopted IFRS S1/S2 and future ISSB standards through NBAA Technical Pronouncement No. 1 of 2024; mandatory use applies to public interest and public-sector entities for financial years beginning on or after 1 Jan 2025.

name: Thailand
hoverText: Thailand's SEC is still consultative. An ISSB roadmap consultation closed in Dec 2024, with follow-on consultation planned on proposed sustainability disclosure requirements; mandatory timing remains unspecified.

name: Turkey
hoverText: Türkiye has fully embedded IFRS S1/S2 into TSRS 1 and TSRS 2. TSRS became mandatory for fiscal periods starting on or after 1 Jan 2024 for listed entities, financial institutions, and other in-scope large entities, with limited reliefs.

name: Uganda
hoverText: Uganda remains in progress. ICPAU's proposed roadmap would permit voluntary reporting from 1 Jan 2026 and phase in mandatory reporting from 2028 to 2030, but the final roadmap had not yet been issued in the reviewed sources.

name: United Kingdom
hoverText: The UK currently uses mandatory TCFD-aligned disclosures under the Companies Act for large companies, FCA listing-rule climate disclosures for listed issuers, and the FCA's SDR regime for asset managers; UK SRS endorsement and broader ISSB implementation remain in progress.

name: Zambia
hoverText: Zambia has adopted IFRS S1 and IFRS S2. Publicly accountable entities must apply them for annual periods beginning on or after 1 Jan 2025, while other entities may use them voluntarily.

name: Zimbabwe
hoverText: Zimbabwe remains in progress. The PAAB allowed early adoption of ISSB standards in 2022, but an adoption roadmap had not yet been issued in the reviewed sources; mandatory timing remains unspecified.
